rulings.Case Title: REZA MOINI NAZERI VS. ALI MOINI NAZERI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for AUG 15, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with final leave to amend all three causes of action: 1)Causes of Action for Quiet Title and Partition. Plaintiff has no current legal interest in the Property and therefore lacks standing. In order to proceed with the action Plaintiff shall allege in good faith facts to unwind/cancel/void the following two transfers: (1) Ocotber 19, 2010 form Shafe to Akbar and (2) October 5, 2010 from Reza to Shafe. Additionally Shafe to be named as a party to the action (as Plaintiff or Defendant) because she is a necessary party due to her (past) interest in the Property. Reza transferred title to Shafe per her request so that "she had an ownership interest.". Reza and Akbar agreed that they would still own the property 50/50 notwithstanding transfer of 100% interest to Shafe on October 5, 2010. There is no allegation that Shafe was a part of this agreement or that she was even on notice of the existence of such agreement made in relation with transfer to Shafe. On October 19, 2010 Shafe, as the sole title owner, transferred title to Akbar. Reza alleges that this transfer was obtained by force and undue influence, but fails to allege facts to support his standing to bring such a claim. 2)Declaratory Relief Cause of action: (a) declaration regarding (i) title and (ii) partition is duplicative of other causes of action for Quiet Title and Partition and cannot be maintained for the same reasons stated above; (b) declaration regarding mortgage - Plaintiff has to allege actual controversy. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MAX M SELVA VS. BENJAMIN CANNON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for AUG 15, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT BENJAMIN CANNON's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND DENIED. There is no new law, facts or circumstances addressing the merits of the underlying motion. Whether the Motion to Vacate has been properly served has not been an issue in this action as Plaintiff filed an opposition to the Motion to Vacate indicating the receipt of such motion. The issue was the merits of the Motion (moving papers were not sufficient to carry the burden as indicated by the Court's tentative ruling requiring a hearing) and whether the order shorting time was obtained per properly noticed ex-parte (lack of ex-parte notice was specifically placed at issue by the opposition). Nothing in the Motion for Reconsideration provides new facts, circumstances or law on regarding the merits or the timing of the Motion to Vacate. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. PATRICK COOKS ET AL
